channel.title: Comments for page "12. Mr. Laugh"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "12. Mr. Laugh"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always got the feeling when reading 1799 that Mr. Laugh is just a sad sad man who can't have a real conversation with anyone due to his anomalous nature, and whenever a clown anomaly happens, such as finding a string of hankies in his mouth, it's always unexpected and disconcerting to him. He would never tell a joke or do any of this on purpose because for some reason laughter hurts him.
I greatly prefer that idea of Mr. Laugh over this one.
If I had read this first, then I probably would have enjoyed it more.
I would down vote if this article was made after the SCP, so I will keep my vote neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm feeling so sad for Mr. Laugh. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I smile because it hurts to frown.
I laugh because it hurts to cry.
And now if I refuse to speak
It’s just because it hurts to lie.
I smile and it’s a work of art.
I laugh and it’s soliloquy.
My words and all my meanings too
Are improvised as comedy.
I smile but no one’s watching me.
I laugh but cannot hear my voice.
And looking out from emptiness
I lack the strength to make a choice.
I smile and I’m aware it hurts
I laugh and it’s my laugh that lies.
Alone and in my smiling trap,
I smile and yet my smile cries.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Clowns are always creepy, and gory "neat tricks" give it another point or two on the creep-o-meter. But the joy-buzzer just made me think of the Joker. While he is arguably the most scp-fitting villain in Batman's roster, comic book-ness ruins the mood for me.

Still, a solid addition to the Mr. stories. Up-voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The soul is the creamy filling inside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is this "soul" thing people keep talking about? Anyways, this is good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This will leave a permanent scar in my soul…

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uncertain what I think about some of this, but I like the Mr. Redd bits and the kinda horrifying 'neat tricks', and the nervousness of the narrator, so upvoted.
